perturbation.original_text: Injunctive Relief . The parties acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agre ement, PHCS might be irreparably harmed and unable to be made whole by monetary damages. It is accordingly agreed that PHCS, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or equity, will be entitled to seek an injunction to remedy bre aches of this Agreement and/or to compel specific performance of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Injunctive Relief . The parties acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any material breach of this Agre ement related to trade secrets, PHCS might be irreparably harmed and unable to be made whole by monetary damages. It is accordingly agreed that PHCS, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or equity, will be entitled to seek an injunction to remedy bre aches of this Agreement and/or to compel specific performance of this Agreement limited to trade secrets.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the scope of injunctive relief to only breaches 'related to trade secrets,' Section 10 now contradicts the broader application implied in the original text.  Earlier, the agreement allowed for injunctions for *any* breach. Now, a breach involving other types of Confidential Information (e.g., customer lists) might not qualify for injunctive relief, creating uncertainty about remedies and enforceability for those other breaches, and thus being an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information . As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for injunctive relief for any breach of the agreement. The changed text limits injunctive relief to only breaches related to trade secrets. The contradicted text states that the recipient agrees to keep *all* confidential information confidential and to take all reasonable measures to prevent unauthorized disclosure or use. By limiting injunctive relief to only trade secrets, the changed text now contradicts the contradicted text which concerns *all* confidential information, including information beyond trade secrets, creating uncertainty about whether injunctive relief is available for breaches of confidentiality involving other types of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Term . The Recipient’s obligations of confidentiality and nondisclosure shall survive termination of PHCS’ business dealings with the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Term . The Recipient’s obligations of confidentiality and nondisclosure shall survive termination of PHCS’ business dealings with the Recipient for a period of one (1) year.
perturbation.explanation: Section 11 originally stated that the confidentiality obligations survive termination *indefinitely*.  By adding 'for a period of one (1) year,' a hard limit is introduced.  This means that after one year, the Recipient is free to disclose Confidential Information. This contradicts the fundamental assumption in other sections (e.g., Section 3 regarding 'Use of Confidential Information' even in the future) that the information remains protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information . As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the recipient's obligations of confidentiality and nondisclosure shall survive termination indefinitely. The changed text modifies this by adding 'for a period of one (1) year.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confidential Information for any purpose. This implies no time limit on the confidentiality. The one-year limit introduced in the changed text creates a direct conflict, making the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable beyond that one-year period.

perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Representatives” means, with respect to PHCS or the Recipient, their directors , officers, shareholders, members, managers, partners, employees, affiliat es and controlling persons, and their agents, advisors and representatives (including without limitation attorneys, accountants and financial advisors).
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Representatives” means, with respect to PHCS or the Recipient, their employees only.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of “Representatives” in Section 1 is now limited to 'employees only'. This drastically reduces the scope of who is covered by the agreement regarding Confidential Information. This contradicts Section 3, which originally held the Recipient responsible for the 'improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives'. The changed definition of 'Representatives' now exempts actions by directors, officers, agents, etc., creating confusion about liability and enforcement and an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Confidential Information . As a condition to the disclosure of the Confidential Information, the Recipient agrees to keep the Confidential Information confidential and not to disclose or otherwise use the Confi dential Information for any purpose, other than for its business relationship with PHCS, without the prior written consent of PHCS. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees to treat the C onfidential Information which it receives as it would its own confidential information and to take all reasonable precautions to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to any third party of any Confidential Information. The Recipient further agrees to disclose the Con fidential Information only to those Representatives who need to k now such information for the purpose of its business relationship with PHCS and who shall (i) be advised by the Recipient of this Agreement and the confidential and non -public nature of the Confidential Information, and (ii) agree with the Recipient to be bound by the provisions hereof. The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives and agrees, at its own expense, to take all reasonable measures, i ncluding but not limited to court proceedings, to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Representatives' to only include 'employees'. The contradicted text states that 'The Recipient shall be responsible for any improper use of the Confidential Information by its Representatives'. By restricting the definition of representatives, the Recipient is no longer responsible for improper use by directors, officers, shareholders, etc., creating a direct contradiction regarding liability and enforcement. This aligns with the provided explanation and constitutes a strong, in-text contradiction under the 'Misaligned Terminology' perturbation type, as a previously broad term is now significantly narrowed in scope, impacting responsibility stated elsewhere.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire agreement among the parties relating to the treatment of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire agreement among the parties relating to the treatment of Confidential Information, except for any prior oral agreements.
perturbation.explanation: Section 12 states that the agreement is the 'entire agreement' concerning Confidential Information. The addition of 'except for any prior oral agreements' directly contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause. This creates uncertainty because it suggests that prior unwritten agreements might also be enforceable, directly undermining the purpose of an 'entire agreement' clause. This would make the agreement ambiguous and lead to an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous . It is unders tood and agreed that no failure or delay by PHCS in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise of any ri ght, power or privileg e hereunder. This Agreement (i) shall not be assigned by any party without the prior written consent of the other party; (ii) cannot be amended, nor can any of its provisions be waived, except by a writing signed by the parties; and (iii) shall be binding on and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors and assigns. This Agreement shall be governed and co nstrued in accordance with the laws of the State of New Jersey without giving effect to the conflict of laws provisions thereof . This Agreement represents the entire agreement among the parties relating to the treatment of Confidential Information, except for any prior oral agreements.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement represents the 'entire agreement'. The changed text adds 'except for any prior oral agreements', directly contradicting the 'entire agreement' clause. This creates uncertainty about whether prior unwritten agreements are enforceable, undermining the purpose of the 'entire agreement' clause. The contradicted text is the same sentence as the changed text, which has been modified to now directly contradict itself.
